seoTitle: Charleys Forest vs Charlton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Charleys Forest and Charlton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Charleys Forest vs Charlton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Charleys Forest and Charlton.
heading: Charleys Forest vs Charlton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Charleys Forest, New South Wales and Charlton, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Charleys Forest has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Charlton (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Charleys Forest or Charlton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Charleys Forest has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Charlton scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Charleys Forest scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Charlton scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Charleys Forest or Charlton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Charleys Forest has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
